reasoning:
The baseline rate of 'Strategic developments' in Honduras has an average of ~8.833 events per 30-day period. For any 30-day rolling window to exceed this average, it must record at least 9 events. If the events are Poisson-distributed, an average of 8.833 translates to a ~52.2% chance of seeing 9 or more events. 

Honduras is approaching a massive general election in late November 2025, which historically spikes political violence, widespread arrests, protests, and strategic security developments (including state mobilization and gang posturing). Because the 360-day reference period (August 2024–August 2025) largely captures the quieter pre-election lull, I expect the target windows in the immediate run-up to the election (August to November) to manifest a sharply higher event rate than this backward-looking average. 

The first target date (August 10) overlaps nearly 75% with the known baseline trend up to August 3; given the upward trajectory heading into the election, it is very likely to exceed the past year's average, meriting a moderately high probability. In strict adherence to the mandate that probabilities for distant horizons must regress toward 0.5, I have systematically tapered the probabilities from an initial high of 58% down to 52% for the farthest post-inauguration date in January 2026.
